---
title: "Dual Boot"
date: 2013-01-14
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by Shoalster on 2013-01-14
I installed 1210 alongside Windows XP  .   My problem started when I couldn't find an option to boot to Windows.  So I downloaded the Start up Manager from software store and that's when I screwed up.   There were choices to make in the start up mgr. and I picked the one that said Windows Media Center because that is my OS .  I was thinking that would give me the OPTION to boot windows on restart.   Instead  it booted to the Windows Recovery screen .  I chose system restore from there and got back to windows but now I can't boot to Ubuntu.  I have Ubuntu on flash drive but when I use the "Try Ubuntu" option and go to the software store it doesn't offer the start-up mgr so I can reverse my selection back to Linux for boot.    Is there a way to get back to my  installed Ubuntu and if so then how do I get option to boot to either system?

---

### Post by oldfred on 2013-01-14
Welcome to the forums.

What did you run from recovery. If just Windows repairs it may have only reinstalled the Windows boot loader, or if more it may have totally reinstalled your Windows. The vendor recovery is an image of hard drive as purchased, the Windows recovery is a Windows repair consolue. But both are called recovery.

This can reinstall grub2 if that is the issue.
       Post the link to the BootInfo report that this creates. Is part of Boot-Repair:
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Boot-Info](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Boot-Info)
Boot Repair -Also handles LVM, GPT, separate /boot and UEFI dual boot.:
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Boot-Repair](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Boot-Repair)
You can repair many boot issues with this or 'Create BootInfo' report (Other Options) & post the link it creates, so we can see your exact configuration and diagnose advanced problems.
Install in Ubuntu liveCD or USB or Full RepairCD with Boot-Repair (for newer computers) 
[http://sourceforge.net/p/boot-repair/home/Home/](http://sourceforge.net/p/boot-repair/home/Home/)
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UbuntuSecureRemix](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UbuntuSecureRemix)

    
I would not use SUM, as it is not maintained and may not work with newer configurations.
       StartUp-Manager is dead  - SUM 2011-05-06
[https://launchpad.net/startup-manager/+announcement/8300](https://launchpad.net/startup-manager/+announcement/8300)

---

OK. Thanks.   To answer your question ,  in windows recovery I chose the Restore option .  It then booted to windows normally and asked if I wanted to restore windows to earlier time and I declined since I was successfully in windows.    I am going to USB boot now to Unbuntu and follow your instructions.  Will post info when completed.   Thanks again.

### Post by Shoalster on 2013-01-14
No luck.  Still booted to Windows Recovery.   Hopefully there is a clue in the boot info.

### Post by YannBuntu on 2013-01-15
Hello
> **Shoalster said:**
> boot repair:  [http://paste.ubuntu.com/1533172/](http://paste.ubuntu.com/1533172/)

GRUB is in your MBR , so you should see the GRUB menu, it's strange you boot directly to Win Recovery.

However, your Ubuntu partition is far (>100GB) from the start of the disk, so you probably need a /boot partition at the start of the disk: [https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BootPartition](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BootPartition)

---

### Post by Shoalster on 2013-01-15
I'm working on it.

---

### Post by Shoalster on 2013-01-15
Followed  YannBuntu's instructions.  Still booting Windows only.   URL after boot repair :    [http://paste.ubuntu.com/1536145/](http://paste.ubuntu.com/1536145/)
   Guess I'll try a new install of Ubuntu.    Can I expect any options when I install over the previous installation that may be asked of me?

### Post by robertthebruce on 2013-01-16
i too could not get ubuntu desktop going reliably on my old lappy, after a few boots it would loose the uuid information(or so it seemed), recover (fsk), then eventually panic and sieze... i 

I remember the "Try Now" or "Install" screen specifically. I tried to many times on different flash drives to forget this...

I got around this by installing Server and then 

> sudo apt-get install ubuntu-desktop

Works great with no drive encrypton and. If uuid was what the problem was it is all working fine now...

...

after installing desktop over server i decided i didnt need all that stuff so i re-installed server and ....

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/ServerGUI](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/ServerGUI)

i choose this path cause i did not have to touch the keyboard much - im saving that for zonemeinder...

hope this helps

---

### Post by YannBuntu on 2013-01-16
> **Shoalster said:**
> Followed  YannBuntu's instructions.  Still booting Windows only.   URL after boot repair :    [http://paste.ubuntu.com/1536145/](http://paste.ubuntu.com/1536145/)

Hi
It looks better, but you created your /boot partition too far from the start of the disc. It must be located inside the 100 first GB of the disk.

---

### Post by Shoalster on 2013-01-17
I thought I was supposed to create it at the start of the partition where unbuntu  resides ( blue arrow). So I guess you mean where the red arrow is?     I really feel stupid since I've been running nothing but Windows ever since Windows 95 and that's like having good driving skills but knowing nothing about the mechanics of the car.     I reinstalled Ubuntu as you may notice in this shot of the disk. And am probably getting myself deeper in trouble messing with these partitions but I'll try creating the boot again there at the red arrow if that is correct because after re-install  it's still booting direct to ubuntu now with no option for Windows.
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/jpeg;base64,[/IMG]

### Post by oldfred on 2013-01-17
You said you were booting Windows only not Ubuntu. If Ubuntu boots then you do not have to make any changes to boot partitions as it works. Only some BIOS will not let you boot to a grub menu if boot files are too far past the first 100GB of a drive.

If Windows does not boot then that is a different set of issues. 

Have you run:
sudo update-grub

---

### Post by Shoalster on 2013-01-17
Yes I was booting to Windows only AFTER I messed with that startup mgr. that I mentioned at the beginning of this thread.  Now that I have reinstalled Ubuntu I'm back to the original problem of not being able to boot windows.   .  I'll run the grub update on this new install and if that doesn't help I'll close the thread.     Thanks for your help.

### Post by oldfred on 2013-01-17
Use the little paperclip icon to attach screenshots or files.

Are you booting from the second entry as your full Windows is in sda2, recovery is in sda1.

---

### Post by Shoalster on 2013-01-17
The computer boots to ubuntu now.  I don't know how to boot windows.

---

### Post by Shoalster on 2013-01-17
This is what it says about sda2
[ATTACH]230202[/ATTACH]

---

### Post by oldfred on 2013-01-17
As it says you need to run chkdsk from a Windows repairCD on sda2.

[http://www.microsoft.com/resources/documentation/windows/xp/all/proddocs/en-us/chkdsk.mspx?mfr=true](http://www.microsoft.com/resources/documentation/windows/xp/all/proddocs/en-us/chkdsk.mspx?mfr=true)

---

### Post by Shoalster on 2013-01-17
Yes I guess I do now.

---
